what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies a conversation-handling bug fix: after new_conversation(), the first turn of the new thread must not feed prior-thread chat history to the model, while within-thread continuity, thread isolation, reopen, reload survival, and legacy global-stream behavior are preserved. It runs model-free by stubbing complete_with_tools and capturing every msgs array passed to the stub.
observations: Stubs the brain and transport so no GPU or endpoint is needed.
Captures every msgs array handed to the stubbed complete_with_tools in a global CAPTURED list.
Uses history_slice to isolate the replayed conversation turns between the system block and the final user message.
Distinguishes prior-thread content from a system recent-activity telemetry ticker.
Asserts on model input, returned history, persisted thread_id, list/reopen, and reload with a fresh Suite.
Includes a faulthandler hang guard and uses temporary directories for isolated stores.
standouts: The bug-discriminating assertion is on the captured model input, not the returned history, because the leak was invisible in returned data.
Explicit boundary: prior-thread content may appear only in the system recent-activity ticker, never as replayed conversation turns.
Reload-survival test is scoped to persisted thread data, noting the current pointer is in-memory by design.
conventions: Uses a module-level PASS counter and a check() helper for assertions.
Heavy inline comments narrate test intent and the bug root cause.
Model-free stubbing via monkeypatch of module-level functions.
Temp directories provide isolated FsStore instances.
concerns: Relies on monkeypatching module-level imports; fragile if chat() imports change.
check() mixes global counting with assertion side effects.
Hard-coded persisted-record counts (e.g., 4 records per 2-turn conversation) depend on implementation details not shown.
References line 4152 for the in-memory current pointer, which may drift.
notes: A model-free acceptance test that catches a thread-history leak by inspecting the exact messages fed to the stubbed LLM.
questions: What is _chat_context / recent_events and how is the 48-char recent-activity ticker generated?
How do new_conversation, load_conversation, current_conversation, list_conversations, and store.chats_in_thread actually work?
What role does the gid argument play in Suite.chat()?
Why does legacy global-stream behavior remain when current_conversation() is None?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to prove fresh-start, continuity, isolation, reopen, reload, and legacy behavior, and the assertions cover each.
apparent_themes: Backward compatibility with global chat stream
intention_signals: The author wants to make the invisible history leak visible and prevent regression by asserting on the exact LLM input array, while documenting that cross-thread recent-activity telemetry is a separate, weaker mechanism.
novelty: Captures the msgs argument to the stubbed brain to assert on the conversation-history slice, exposing a bug that the returned history hides.
contribution: Provides a deterministic, no-endpoint regression test that captures LLM input arrays and verifies thread isolation, fresh-start, continuity, reopen, reload survival, and legacy global-stream semantics for conversation handling.
summary_for_embedding: Model-free acceptance test for conversation thread isolation that stubs the LLM, captures the messages array fed to the model, and asserts a new conversation starts fresh without leaking prior-thread turns.
intention_for_embedding: To regression-test the fix for Tim's bug where a new conversation's first reply reused prior-thread context because the model input used global chat history.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints ok/fail.
symbols.description: Takes a label and condition, asserts the condition with a prefixed failure message, increments the module-level PASS counter, and prints a success marker.

symbols.does: Builds a new Suite over a fresh FsStore and installs model-free stubs.
symbols.description: Creates a Suite instance backed by the given store directory, registers discovered nodes, and calls _install_stubs to replace the brain and transport with deterministic test doubles.

symbols.does: Monkeypatches capability probe, brain call, and transport so chat() runs without a live model.
symbols.description: Forces tool support to true, replaces fabric.client.complete_with_tools with a stub that captures msgs and returns a fixed reply, replaces the transport builder with a no-op factory, and sets a listening-mode RHM config so chat() stays on the normal path.

symbols.does: Test stub that records the messages array and returns a fixed text reply.
symbols.description: Appends a deep copy of the msgs argument to the global CAPTURED list and returns a deterministic assistant response with no tool calls.

symbols.does: Extracts the conversation-history turns from a captured msgs array.
symbols.description: Returns all messages between the first (system) and last (final user) messages, representing the replayed prior turns fed to the model.

symbols.does: Joins message contents into a single string for easy substring checks.
symbols.description: Concatenates the content field of each message with newlines so assertions can search the combined text.

symbols.does: Runs the full acceptance scenario and returns 0 on success.
symbols.description: Creates temporary stores, exercises two threads, asserts fresh-start, continuity, isolation, list/reopen, reload survival, and legacy global-stream behavior using the captured model inputs, then prints the pass count.
